core_mechanism: a bond-percolation / edge-transmission spreading process on a network, in which each link independently transmits with some (possibly non-uniform, correlated) probability and a sharp critical threshold separates a regime where the affected set stays negligible from one where a giant connected cluster spanning a finite fraction of nodes forms — made exactly solvable via probability generating functions over the network's degree/connection structure
brief_reason: The abstract reduces a class of spreading models with per-pair (non-uniform, correlated) transmission probabilities to something exactly solvable on arbitrary networks, which is precisely the mapping of edge-by-edge stochastic spreading onto bond percolation with a critical outbreak threshold.
